abstract: The International Pearl Millet Smut Nursery(IPMSN) in one of the component nurseries in the International Pearl Millet Disease Resistance Testing Program (IPMDRTP) cooridinated by the ICRISAT Pearl Millet Improvement Program. Through the assistance of cooperators in diverse loactions throughout the semi-arid tropics the IPMDRTP aims to identify sources of disease resistance that is stable across pathogen population and environments. The iPMSN was initinated in 1977 with the participation of cooperators in Senegal, Nigeria, and India and five promising lines were identified. In 1978 the IPMSN was sent to cooperators at 12 locations in 7 countries and the results were received from 6 locations in 4 countries. Ten of the 34 entries were identified promising with across locations mean smut severity of not more than 10 percent. In 1979 the 37 entry IPMSN was sent to cooperators at 6 locations in 5 countries and the  were identified as highly resistant with across IPMSN was sent to cooperators at 8 locations in 4 countries and results received from 6 locations in 3 countries are presented in this report
institution: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics
publisher: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics
keywords: Pennisetum americanum; Tolyposporium penicillariae; Nurseries; Plant disease control; Nigeria; India; Senegal, RP4276
note: This report was compiled by Dr R.P.Thakur, K.V. Subba Rao, and R.J.Willams Plant Pathologist, Technical Assistant and Principal pathologist respectively, Millet Improvement Program, ICRISAT. They are highly indebted to all the cooperators who gave so much of their time and facilities tp provide the data used in this report
title: Report on the 1980 International Pearl Millet Smut Nursery (IPMSN)
